description: Modeling the same concept differently across endpoints forces clients to write per-endpoint code and makes the surface unpredictable.
body: Failure mode: the same domain concept appears under different names, casings, or shapes on different endpoints because each was designed in isolation. Detection signals: a client maps the same entity twice with different field names; reviewers cannot predict an endpoint's shape from another; the style guide is absent or unenforced. Do not design endpoints independently of the existing surface. Safer fallback: define one convention and a canonical model per concept, reuse names, and converge outliers additively with deprecation rather than ad hoc renames.
